meta_description: Decode your .PL8 files (+ .256 palette) to PNG, right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.
hero_subtitle: Drop a .PL8 file and its .256 palette: decoding runs entirely in your browser. Your files never leave your machine.
about_intro: The .PL8 is a paged sprite container used by mid-1990s DOS games, notably those developed by Impressions Games and published by Sierra. A single file holds many images — units, buildings, UI elements — each described by a header (dimensions, pixel-data offset).
about_palette: Colors aren't stored inside the .PL8: each pixel is an index into an external palette, the .256 file (256 colors in 6-bit VGA). Index 0 means transparent — which is why the tool needs both files.
enc_bitmap: Simple bitmap — one byte per pixel, read straight as a palette index.
enc_rle: RLE — run-length compression: a count of opaque pixels, or skips of transparent ones.
enc_iso: Isometric — centered diamond rows (width varies with height), with optional "extra rows" for building tops.
about_local: Decoding (palette mapping, RLE, isometric geometry, trimming) runs entirely in your browser: no upload, no server.
copyright_note: ⚠️ This tool ships no game assets. You bring your own files, extracted from a copy you own.
err_need_both: Load a .PL8 file and a .256 palette first.
err_not_ready: The decoding engine isn't wired in yet (see src/decode.js).
